description: Record Python interpreter version and platform.
Check whether compression.zstd is importable in this Python build.
compression.zstd.compress/decompress roundtrip, exact byte equality.
Empty input roundtrip via compression.zstd. Compressed representation need not be empty.
Compress identical input twice with identical options; record whether bytes/hash match locally. Not a universal promise.
Two modest compression levels. Record sizes, verify roundtrip. Do not assert higher level must shrink tiny input.
input_bytes: the local compressor test repeats repeats repeats

description: ZstdCompressor.compress() with CONTINUE, finish FLUSH_FRAME. Concatenate chunks, verify decompressed output.
input_bytes: taco salsa lime tortilla racket serve court volley

description: Exercise FLUSH_BLOCK then complete frame. Verify final roundtrip. Intermediate calls may return empty bytes.
input_bytes: bonjour sujet identique mais vocabulaire different

description: Two independent frames concatenated. Module-level decompress() should return concatenation of both payloads when supported.
Feed concatenated frames to ZstdDecompressor. Record eof, unused_data behavior locally. Do not claim transparent multi-frame processing.
Bounded decompression with small max_length, continue with empty input while needs_input is false. Verify full reconstruction.
input_bytes: taco salsa lime tortilla racket serve court volley

description: set_pledged_input_size() with exact size before frame. Verify roundtrip succeeds.
input_bytes: the local compressor test repeats repeats repeats

description: Deliberately pledge incorrect small size. Record real exception type/message. Expected_error only when mismatch hits intended path.
input_bytes: taco salsa lime tortilla racket serve court volley

description: Enable checksum_flag, flip a byte in checksum area, verify ZstdError is raised on decompress. Preserve exception evidence.
input_bytes: bonjour sujet identique mais vocabulaire different

description: io.BytesIO with ZstdFile or compression.zstd.open(). No permanent filesystem writes.
ZstdDict is_raw=True with small synthetic dictionary. Compress/decompress with same dict. Does not claim improvement for every input.
Demonstrate only that incremental compressor state changes as data is supplied. Must not call this learning/training/classification.
input_bytes: the local compressor test repeats repeats repeats

description: zlib.compressobj(zdict=...) and Compress.copy() where available. Audit HN correction that dict/state-copy predated Python 3.14. Do not claim zlib/Zstandard APIs identical.
Four tiny samples: similar vocab/similar topic; similar vocab/different topic; different vocab/similar topic; longer text. Record compressed sizes only. No accuracy %, no semantic claim.
Context-only case confirming README/RESULTS contain narrow disclaimer, do not claim compression is intelligent/semantic/universally accurate/suitable for production classification.
